title: Default Arguments
description: Make parameters optional with default values.

slides.title: Optional Data 🤔
slides.content:
## Required by Default

Normally, when you define a parameter like `name: String`, calling the function without providing a name will cause an immediate crash. Kotlin demands all arguments.

But what if you want to make a parameter **optional**?

slides.title: Default Values 🛡️
slides.content:
You can assign a default fallback value directly inside the parameter list using `=`. 

If the user provides an argument, it overwrites the default. If they provide nothing, the default is used automatically!

quizData.question: If you call `displayAlert()` with absolutely NO arguments, what happens?
quizData.explanation: Because the `message: String` parameter does NOT have a default value, it is strictly required. The compiler will complain that one argument is missing.

slides.title: Java Overloading vs Kotlin ✨
slides.content:
## Cleaner Code

In older languages like Java, to achieve this behavior, you had to write the function multiple times (once accepting 1 argument, once accepting 2). This is called Method Overloading.

Kotlin's default arguments destroy boilerplate code, allowing one function to handle dozens of combinations.
